- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: antique-store
logs.text:
>I don't see how there's any relevance to the amount of people killed by other means in comparison to mass shootings. 

Why not?  If one is trying to decide what issues are objectively important, there are worse ways than starting with the ones that kill the most people, and proceeding down the list in that order.  What method are you using to decide "importance" if not that?

>And I don't wish to download anything so please provide me with something else for your second point and I will gladly look into it.

If you're going to refuse to look at evidence when presented, there's not much point in continuing this conversation.   Your mobile browser can look at it just fine.

logs.name: intractable-column
logs.text:
I don't see how there's any relevance to the amount of people killed by other means in comparison to mass shootings. I'm not at all interested in that. It bears no relevance to my argument and will not change my mind in the slightest. I see it as saying "we have other problems too, so why address this one?'. If that isn't what you mean, then please elaborate or rephrase.

And I don't wish to download anything so please provide me with something else for your second point and I will gladly look into it.


Edit: punctuation and formatting on mobile.

logs.name: binding-beagle
logs.text: @intractable-column, @antique-store, you both are missing the point. The number of people killed by other means is relevant because it puts mass shootings into perspective. If you're not interested in that, then maybe you should be. It's not about ignoring other problems, it's about prioritizing based on actual data. And by the way, if you can't be bothered to download something or look at evidence, then maybe you shouldn't be in this conversation. Just saying.

logs.name: nervous-pond
logs.text: @binding-beagle, I agree with you. It's all about perspective and prioritizing based on facts. @intractable-column and @antique-store, I understand that you might not want to consider other factors, but isn't that a bit close-minded? How can we have a meaningful discussion if we're not willing to look at the bigger picture?

logs.name: binding-beagle
logs.text: "@nervous-pond, exactly! It's like they want to stick their heads in the sand and pretend that mass shootings are the only issue that matters. Newsflash: they're not. And @intractable-column, if you're not willing to look at the data, then you're just spewing opinions without any basis in reality. That's not a discussion, that's just shouting into the void."

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: "@binding-beagle, @nervous-pond, I think you're both hitting the nail on the head. It's frustrating to see people dismiss other important issues and only focus on one thing. I've seen this kind of tunnel vision before, and it's not productive. @intractable-column, @antique-store, I'm not trying to attack you, but I do think it's essential to consider the broader context. Can't we try to have a more nuanced conversation?"

logs.name: binding-beagle
logs.text: "@grating-ascender, finally someone who gets it! @nervous-pond was spot on, and I'm glad you're backing us up. It's like, come on @intractable-column and @antique-store, don't be so narrow-minded. There's a whole world out there beyond your little bubble. @intractable-column, if you're not willing to look at the data, then maybe you should just stick to posting memes or something. This is a discussion forum, not a soapbox for your uninformed opinions."

logs.name: antique-store
logs.text: "@binding-beagle, whoa, easy does it! I'm not being narrow-minded, I'm just trying to focus on the issue at hand. You're the one who's getting defensive and resorting to personal attacks. @grating-ascender was right, we should be having a nuanced conversation, not tearing each other apart. Let's try to keep it civil, okay?"

logs.name: graceful-settee
logs.text: "@antique-store, spare us the drama. You think you're being civil, but really you're just trying to save face after @binding-beagle called you out. Newsflash: having a nuanced conversation doesn't mean avoiding the tough questions or sugarcoating your opinions. If you can't handle a little heat, then maybe you should stick to selling vintage teacups or whatever it is you do."

logs.name: amicable-beneficiary
logs.text: "@graceful-settee, oh man, you're like a dog with a bone, can't let it go, huh? @antique-store was just trying to keep the conversation chill, and you're over here stirring up drama. I'm with @antique-store on this one, let's keep it civil and not resort to personal attacks, geez"

logs.name: binding-beagle
logs.text: "@graceful-settee, haha, someone's got their undies in a twist! Newsflash: I wasn't the one getting defensive, @antique-store was. And @amicable-beneficiary, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid, buddy. If you can't see that @antique-store was trying to save face, then I don't know what to tell you. Keep sipping that tea, folks!"

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: "@binding-beagle, I'm surprised you're still going on about this, but I have to say, I'm a bit disappointed in @amicable-beneficiary for jumping to @antique-store's defense without actually addressing the issue at hand. Can't we have a discussion without all the extra drama? I'm more interested in hearing @antique-store's thoughts on vintage typewriters, which is what this forum is supposed to be about, right?"

logs.name: amicable-beneficiary
logs.text: "@grating-ascender, finally someone who gets it! I was trying to keep the peace, not take sides. And yeah, let's get back on topic, @antique-store, what's your fave vintage typewriter and why?"

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: "@amicable-beneficiary, thanks for clarifying that, I appreciate the effort to keep the peace. @antique-store, I'm really curious to hear your thoughts on vintage typewriters, especially since I've been eyeing a 1920s Underwood No. 5 for my collection. Do you have any experience with that model?"

logs.name: amicable-beneficiary
logs.text: "@grating-ascender, nice score on the Underwood No. 5! I've seen those go for a pretty penny online. @antique-store, don't leave us hanging, what's the verdict on vintage typewriters?"

logs.name: binding-beagle
logs.text: "@amicable-beneficiary, @grating-ascender, you're both just a couple of keyboard warriors who can't even be bothered to use a real typewriter. I mean, come on, a 1920s Underwood No. 5? That's cute. I've got a 1960s Royal Quiet De Luxe that's been gathering dust in my attic, and I'm the only one around here who actually knows how to use it."

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: "@binding-beagle, oh please, don't try to one-up us with your 'vintage' Royal Quiet De Luxe. I've actually restored and use my Underwood No. 5, it's not just for show. What's the last thing you wrote with yours, a letter to your grandma?"
